critical_findings.issue: NO FORGOT PASSWORD / RESET PASSWORD FLOW EXISTS. The login page has no 'Forgot Password' link. No /reset-password route exists in App.js. No resetPasswordForEmail function found in SupabaseAuthContext.js. User has NO self-service password recovery path.
critical_findings.reproduction: 1. Navigate to /login-supabase. 2. Observe there is no 'Forgot Password' link below the password field or anywhere on the page. 3. Search codebase: grep -rn 'forgot|reset.*pass' pages/LoginSupabase.js returns 0 results.
critical_findings.root_cause: Feature never implemented. Supabase Auth supports resetPasswordForEmail() natively but it was never wired into the frontend.
critical_findings.impact: Users locked out of accounts permanently if password is forgotten. Only recovery path is direct Supabase admin intervention.

critical_findings.issue: biqc-insights-cognitive Edge Function returns HTTP 401 on EVERY call from production. Console logs show: 'Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 401 () at https://uxyqpdfftxpkzeppqtvk.supabase.co/functions/v1/biqc-insights-cognitive'. This means the Edge Function is rejecting the access token.
critical_findings.reproduction: 1. Login to biqc.ai. 2. Navigate to /market or /advisor. 3. Open browser console. 4. Observe repeated 401 errors from biqc-insights-cognitive.
critical_findings.root_cause: Possible: (a) Edge Function not deployed with latest code, (b) Supabase auth JWT verification failing, (c) ANON_KEY mismatch between frontend and Edge Function, (d) Edge Function expects service_role token but receives user token.
critical_findings.impact: The entire cognitive engine is non-functional on production. Market page falls back to /api/market-intelligence backend endpoint. Action Plan, competitive signals, industry trends — all from Edge Function — are unavailable.

high_findings.issue: Backend /api/auth/supabase/me returns HTTP 520 (Cloudflare 'Web server returning unknown error'). This is the user profile endpoint used by ProtectedRoute for admin checks.
high_findings.reproduction: Console logs show: 'Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 520 () at https://biqc.ai/api/auth/supabase/me'. Multiple occurrences per page load.
high_findings.root_cause: Backend server may be hitting an unhandled exception or timeout when processing the /me endpoint. The 520 is a Cloudflare error indicating the origin server responded with something unexpected.
high_findings.impact: Admin role detection may fail intermittently. Users may not see admin-specific features (Super Admin panel, Forensic Calibration access).

high_findings.issue: Market page remains on CognitiveMesh loading animation for >12 seconds on production. API calls to /api/market-intelligence, /api/integrations/channels/status, /api/forensic/calibration show ERR_ABORTED in console — suggesting they are being cancelled before completing.
high_findings.reproduction: 1. Login. 2. Navigate to /market. 3. Wait 12+ seconds. 4. Page still shows 'You are about to see how your market currently sees you' loading animation.
high_findings.root_cause: The Promise.allSettled parallel calls may be getting cancelled by React component unmounting during auth re-bootstrap. The 8-second timeout fires and sets loading=false, but if no API call succeeded, snapshot is null and the page shows loading state content without actual data.
high_findings.impact: Market page appears broken to users. The primary intelligence surface is non-functional on production.

high_findings.issue: Login page right panel (Sovereign Intelligence trust panel) is hidden with lg:flex — correct behavior. However, the login form panel uses flex-1 which means on mobile it takes full width but has excessive top padding (py-12 + empty space). The form content starts ~200px from the top of the viewport, wasting 25% of mobile screen.
high_findings.reproduction: 1. Open /login-supabase on 390px viewport. 2. Observe ~200px empty space above 'Back to home' link.
high_findings.root_cause: The flex-1 + py-12 + justify-center creates excessive vertical centering on mobile where the right panel is hidden.
high_findings.impact: Users on mobile must scroll or strain to see the login form. Reduces perceived professionalism.

medium_findings.issue: Mobile bottom navigation (MobileNav component) exists in DOM (data-testid=mobile-bottom-nav confirmed) but is not visible in the Playwright screenshot rendering. This is likely a screenshot tool limitation (renders at 1456px regardless of viewport setting). The lg:hidden class should correctly hide it on desktop and show on mobile. REQUIRES REAL DEVICE VERIFICATION.
medium_findings.reproduction: Test on actual Android device at 390px width.
medium_findings.root_cause: Cannot fully verify mobile rendering with current screenshot tool. DOM presence confirmed.
medium_findings.impact: If bottom nav fails to render on real devices, mobile navigation remains sidebar-only.

medium_findings.issue: OAuth flows (Google, Microsoft) use full redirect (skipBrowserRedirect: false) — correct for mobile. However, callback page hardcodes navigate('/advisor') without checking mobile viewport or system_state. Mobile landing logic (Module A) was implemented in AuthCallbackSupabase.js but only on the non-OAuth codepath. The OAuth callback path at line 133 still hardcodes /advisor.
medium_findings.reproduction: 1. Click 'Continue with Google' on mobile. 2. Complete OAuth. 3. Returns to /advisor regardless of system_state.
medium_findings.root_cause: Mobile landing logic (DRIFT→/market) was added only to email+password flow path, not the OAuth callback path.
medium_findings.impact: Users logging in via OAuth on mobile always land on /advisor, never /market, regardless of system_state.

medium_findings.issue: After logout, the redirect goes to root '/' (homepage) rather than '/login-supabase'. The DashboardLayout handleSignOut uses window.location.href = '/login-supabase' but the actual redirect observed was to '/'. This may be because the Supabase signOut triggers a session change that routes through PublicRoute which renders the homepage.
medium_findings.reproduction: 1. Login. 2. Click user avatar → Sign Out. 3. Observe redirect to '/' not '/login-supabase'.
medium_findings.root_cause: signOut() clears session → auth state change triggers PublicRoute re-evaluation → PublicRoute renders children (homepage) since user is now unauthenticated.
medium_findings.impact: User lands on marketing homepage instead of login page. Minor UX confusion.

low_findings.issue: Tutorial modal ('BIQC INSIGHTS — Your Strategic Advisor') appears on every first page load, blocking content. No 'Don't show again' option visible. Users must dismiss it on every fresh session.
low_findings.reproduction: 1. Login. 2. Land on /advisor. 3. Tutorial modal blocks the dashboard.
low_findings.root_cause: Tutorial overlay persistence may not be storing dismissal state correctly.

low_findings.issue: Edge Function warmup call fires on app load (App.js useEffect) but uses bare apikey without user auth. The biqc-insights-cognitive function requires auth — warmup returns 401. The warmup is effectively a no-op that wastes a network request.
low_findings.reproduction: Console shows: warmup call to biqc-insights-cognitive returns 401.
low_findings.root_cause: Warmup in App.js sends apikey only, but the Edge Function validates user token.
low_findings.impact: Wasted network request on every app load. Cold start not actually mitigated.

auth_flow_analysis.note: Cannot test actual OAuth flow in Playwright
Cannot test actual OAuth flow in Playwright
No error shown for correct credentials

reset_password_flow_analysis.evidence: No 'Forgot Password' link on /login-supabase. No route for /reset-password in App.js. No resetPasswordForEmail in codebase.

issues: Content may never render on slow connections

routing_logic_analysis.actual: NOT TESTABLE — mobile landing logic in AuthCallbackSupabase is for email login only, screenshot tool renders desktop

routing_logic_analysis.scenario: Direct URL to /market while logged out

session_integrity_analysis.test: Back button does not restore session

edge_latency_observations.impact: CRITICAL — cognitive engine non-functional on production

edge_latency_observations.note: Edge function created in codebase but not yet deployed to Supabase
App.js warmup uses apikey only — Edge Function requires user auth, so warmup is ineffective
